data.name: TR-PICUS-TIER1-REGISTRY_PERSISTENCE_RUNKEYS: Registry Run/RunOnce Persistence Creation
data.description: CRITICAL BEHAVIORAL DETECTION: Detects registry persistence via Run/RunOnce key creation, a primary post-exploitation persistence mechanism observed in 35+ Picus detonation instances. Monitors for suspicious registry modifications to HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run(Once) and HKLM equivalents from non-standard processes. This is the #1 persistence pattern used by threat actors. Observable from Picus detonation: reg.exe, cmd.exe, and powershell.exe modifying these keys in non-administrative context. Expected to catch 35+ undetected persistence attempts.
data.contents: ((registry.key_path contains 'Software\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\Run' OR registry.key_path contains 'Software\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\RunOnce') AND (process.name contains 'powershell.exe' OR process.name contains 'cmd.exe' OR process.name contains 'reg.exe' OR process.name contains 'regedit.exe') AND (process.parent.name != 'msiexec.exe' AND process.parent.name != 'explorer.exe' AND process.parent.name != 'rundll32.exe' AND process.parent.name != 'regsvcs.exe')) OR ((registry.key_path contains 'Software\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\Run' OR registry.key_path contains 'Software\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\RunOnce') AND registry.value_data contains '.exe' AND process.integrity_level != 'system' AND process.integrity_level != 'high')

techniques.name: Boot or Logon Autostart Execution: Registry Run Keys / Startup Folder

data.expectedCoverage: 35+ undetected persistence instances from Picus detonation
